reasoning: Egypt has demonstrated strong support for international sporting events with heavy government backing. Recently, Egypt's national traditional karate team won 1st place overall at the 2024 ITKF World Championship, establishing them as a premier nation in the sport. During the African Open Championship held in Cairo in late April 2025, Egyptian Minister of Youth and Sports Ashraf Sobhi met with the ITKF President to discuss hosting the 2026 World Championship. This active lobbying, combined with Egypt's strong capacity to host, makes them a very likely candidate to win the hosting rights. Small international federations like the ITKF often welcome government-backed bids and can process them relatively quickly. Considering the event is in 2026, the host needs to be announced soon (typically 1–1.5 years in advance). The main uncertainty is whether the official announcement happens before the tight deadline of July 1, 2025 (about two months after the discussion). However, the strong initial bid and the alignment of interests make a prompt announcement solid, so I estimate a 62% chance of the resolution conditions being met by July 1.
